meta.schema_ref: plugin:UnrealAiEditor/Resources/UnrealAiToolCatalog.json (single embedded catalog, no separate schema file)

invocation.focused_flag: Optional JSON field "focused": true on supported tools. Stripped before handlers; after a successful run the editor opens or foregrounds the related Blueprint, asset, Content Browser selection, or project source file in the IDE. Tools list this property explicitly where the catalog schema is strict (additionalProperties: false).

tools.summary: Attach child actor to parent.

child_path.description: World path of the child actor (same as child_actor_path).

parent_path.description: World path of the parent actor (same as parent_actor_path).

actor_path.description: When paired with parent_path or parent_actor_path, treated as the child actor path.

tools.summary: Destroy an actor in the editor world.

confirm.description: Assent to delete: DELETE, yes, y, true, confirm, or 1 (case-insensitive).

tools.summary: Find actors by outliner label, internal actor name, full object path substring, or exact actor_path match.
Read actor transform and mobility.

Set actor transform. Omit `transform` and top-level location/rotation/scale to keep the current pose (useful with `teleport_physics` only). Use `transform` object, or top-level `location`/`rotation`/`scale` number arrays [x,y,z]. `actor_path` accepts full path or a unique suffix (e.g. PersistentLevel.Actor_UAID_...).

transform.description: Optional if using top-level location[]/rotation[]/scale[] instead. Arrays inside transform also accepted ([x,y,z] form).

location.description: Optional when transform omitted: [x,y,z] applied with current rotation/scale.

rotation.description: Optional when transform omitted: [pitch,yaw,roll] with current location/scale.

scale.description: Optional when transform omitted: [x,y,z] scale with current location/rotation.

tools.summary: Toggle actor visibility.
Toggle whether the actor is temporarily hidden in the editor (flips current state). Prefer actor_set_visibility when you need an explicit hidden true/false.
Spawn an actor in the editor world.

class_path.description: Must be a subclass of AActor (e.g. /Game/MyActor.MyActor_C or /Script/Engine.StaticMeshActor). Components (USceneComponent, etc.) cannot be spawned as actors.

tools.summary: Summarize AnimBlueprint graphs.

focused.description: After success, open this AnimBlueprint in the editor.

tools.summary: HTTP fetch helper (high risk). Call when the user requests this tool by name; use minimal args if unspecified. Handler may return not_implemented until wired.
Spawn OS process (high risk). Call when the user names this tool; minimal args ok. Handler may return not_implemented until wired.
Python eval hook (high risk). Call when the user names this tool; use `{}` if no code given. Handler may return not_implemented until wired.
Delete assets.
Duplicate asset to new path.
Read asset metadata and dependencies summary.
Import files via automated import pipeline.
Open an asset in its native editor when you already have an exact object path (for example from asset_index_fuzzy_search or asset_registry_query). Use for inspection/focus, not discovery.

object_path.description: Canonical asset object path. Preferred key. Example: /Game/Blueprints/MyBP.MyBP.

focused.description: After success, foreground the asset editor (redundant with tool behavior if already opening).

tools.summary: Query Asset Registry for deterministic listing by exact path/class filters. Use this when you need predictable structured results; use asset_index_fuzzy_search for fuzzy name discovery.

path_filter.description: Package path prefix (for example /Game or /Game/Blueprints). Preferred key.

object_path.description: Compatibility alias: object path converted to package path for path_filter.

class_name.description: Single class path filter (for example /Script/Engine.Blueprint). Preferred key.

class_paths.description: Compatibility alias for class_name; first entry is used.

class_filters.description: Compatibility alias for class_name; first entry is used.

class_names.description: Compatibility alias for class_name; first entry is used. Short names are expanded to /Script/Engine.<Name>.

filters.description: Compatibility alias for structured callers. Primary shape is array entries like {"key":"class","value":"/Script/Engine.Blueprint"} and {"key":"path","value":"/Game"}; object shape with path_prefix/path_filter is also tolerated at runtime.

tools.summary: Rename/move asset and optionally fix references.
Save dirty packages.
Preview sound in editor.
Add member variable to Blueprint.

type.description: Variable type token (preferred key). Alias: variable_type.

focused.description: After success, open this Blueprint in the editor.

tools.summary: Compile a Blueprint and return diagnostics. Use format_graphs to run UnrealBlueprintFormatter on script graphs before compile when graphs should be laid out.

format_graphs.description: When true, run Unreal Blueprint Formatter on all non-empty ubergraph, function, and macro graphs before compiling. Requires the UnrealBlueprintFormatter plugin (declared dependency of Unreal AI Editor).

focused.description: After success, open and foreground this Blueprint in the editor.

tools.summary: Apply compact Blueprint IR and materialize/wire EventGraph nodes. Primary tool for gameplay logic (overlap, timers, variables, calls). Use create_if_missing + parent_class for new BPs under /Game; pair with blueprint_compile. Minimal valid shape: blueprint_path + nodes[] + links[] where each node has node_id/op and each link has from/to pin refs. Not for UMG Designer layout.

blueprint_path.description: Blueprint asset path under /Game. Accepted forms: /Game/Folder/MyBP or /Game/Folder/MyBP.MyBP.

create_if_missing.description: When true and blueprint_path is not on disk yet, create an Actor-based Blueprint at that path before applying IR (must be under /Game).

parent_class.description: Optional native class path when using create_if_missing (default /Script/Engine.Actor).

focused.description: After success, open this Blueprint and focus graph_name when set.

merge_policy.description: How to combine with existing graphs. append_to_existing: reuse the first matching ReceiveBeginPlay/ReceiveTick (etc.) in the target graph and wire new exec from the exec tail (warns on duplicate anchors). create_new: always spawn IR nodes. Default: append_to_existing on ubergraph EventGraph, create_new on function/macro graphs.

layout_scope.description: When auto_layout is true: ir_nodes runs formatter on IR materialized nodes only; full_graph runs LayoutEntireGraph on the whole target graph. Default ir_nodes.

auto_layout.description: When true (default), run Unreal Blueprint Formatter after wiring when every IR node has x,y at 0. Requires the UnrealBlueprintFormatter plugin; if missing, apply still succeeds and formatter_hint explains how to enable it.

nodes.description: IR nodes to materialize. Minimal node object shape: {"node_id":"id","op":"event_begin_play"}.

node_id.description: Stable node reference used by links/defaults.

links.description: Connections using pin refs in node_id.pin format.

from.description: Source pin reference in node_id.pin form (example: beginplay.Then).

to.description: Target pin reference in node_id.pin form (example: delay.execute).

tools.summary: Run Unreal Blueprint Formatter LayoutEntireGraph on a Blueprint script graph (readability pass). Requires UnrealBlueprintFormatter; no-op with formatter_hint if missing.

graph_name.description: Optional; default is the first ubergraph page.

focused.description: After success, open this Blueprint and focus the graph when set.

tools.summary: Export bounded summary of a Blueprint graph.

focused.description: After success, open this Blueprint (and graph when named).

tools.summary: Open Blueprint editor focused on a graph.
Line/sphere trace in editor world.
Execute allow-listed console command.
Navigate Content Browser to a folder.
Focus Content Browser on an asset or folder.

focused.description: After success, also open the asset editor for this path when it is an object path.

tools.summary: Cook content for a platform (UAT).
Delete files by path (destructive). Call when the user names this tool; use `{}` if paths unspecified. Handler may enforce project scope.
Return the current editor actor selection (paths and labels); may be empty. Use when the user asks what is selected or before transforms that depend on selection.

include_non_actor.description: Reserved for future non-actor UObject selection rows; currently ignored—only selected AActors are returned.

tools.summary: Switch editor mode (Place, Landscape, ... allow-listed).
Replace editor selection with specified actors.
Read a deterministic editor/world snapshot (JSON). Use when context may be stale or before risky edits; optional asset/viewport slices via flags.

include_assets.description: If true, include summarized asset/Content Browser context when implemented.

include_viewport.description: If true, include viewport/camera hints when implemented.

tools.summary: Read recent Message Log / log tail.
tools.ue_entry_points: FMessageLogModule or FFileHelper on project log

tools.summary: Add foliage instances in radius.
Bring a registered dock tab to front.
Import heightmap to landscape.
List referencers for a material.
Set scalar on material instance.
Set vector parameter on material instance.
Execute a pre-registered editor command ID.
Open MetaSound source.
Move actors to an outliner folder.
Full package build.
Execute PCG graph in editor.
Apply impulse (PIE-oriented).
Start Play-In-Editor. Optional mode: "viewport" (default, in-process PIE) or "standalone" (new process when supported). Pair with pie_stop when finished.

mode.description: viewport = in-process PIE (default). standalone = separate process when supported.

tools.summary: Return PIE / play-session state: playing_in_editor, play_session_request_queued, play_session_in_progress.
Stop current PIE session.
Read text file under project (Source/Config/Content/Plugins, docs/, .github/, scripts/, tools/, build/, root README/LICENSE-style files, *.uproject).

relative_path.description: Project-relative file path (preferred key). Alias: file_path.

focused.description: After success, open this file in the configured source IDE (best-effort).

tools.summary: Write text file under project.

focused.description: After success, open the written file in the configured source IDE (best-effort).

tools.summary: Execute a raw console/exec string (high risk). Call when the user names this tool; `{}` ok—handler may delegate or return not_implemented.
Capture a UTextureRenderTarget2D to disk.
Open Level Sequence in Sequencer.
Wait for shader compile completion.

Fuzzy grep project text files under Source/, Config/, and Plugins/*/Source/ (Cursor-style): typo-tolerant; ranks paths and filenames even when the user misspells or only partially remembers a name. Returns path_matches plus optional line_matches inside files. For Content assets under /Game, use asset_index_fuzzy_search (Asset Registry index).

query.description: Approximate text; may be misspelled. Matched against relative paths, filenames, and (optionally) lines.

glob.description: Semicolon-separated extension globs, e.g. *.cpp;*.h;*.hpp;*.cs. Defaults to common C++ and config extensions.

max_hits.description: Max path_matches returned (line_matches budget scales with this).

max_files_to_scan.description: Cap files opened for line-level fuzzy matching (highest path scores first).

include_line_matches.description: If true, scan text inside top-ranked files for fuzzy line hits.

tools.summary: Fuzzy-search actors in the loaded editor world/level (not Content Browser assets). Use this first when you need to discover actor paths by topic/label before calling actor mutation tools.

query.description: Approximate text; may be misspelled. Compared against actor label, name, class, path, and tags.

max_results.description: Max actors returned (sorted by fuzzy score, best first).

include_hidden.description: If true, include actors hidden in the editor.

tools.summary: Fuzzy-search the Unreal Asset Registry index for asset discovery. Use this first to discover asset paths by approximate name, then pass selected object_path into tools like asset_open_editor/asset_apply_properties.

query.description: Approximate text; may be misspelled. Compared against asset name, object path, and class path. Preferred key. Example: "coin", "footstep", "M_".

search_string.description: Legacy alias for query. Prefer query; accepted for compatibility with older prompts.

filter.description: Compatibility alias for query (deprecated). Prefer query.

name_prefix.description: Compatibility alias for query (deprecated). Prefer query.

path_prefix.description: Package path prefix to scope the registry query, e.g. /Game or /Game/MyFolder. Defaults to /Game.

max_results.description: Max assets returned after ranking by fuzzy score.

max_assets_to_scan.description: Safety cap on assets pulled from the registry before filtering (large projects).

class_name_substring.description: Optional filter: asset class path must contain this substring (e.g. Texture2D, StaticMesh, SoundBase, Material).

tools.summary: Spawn child agent with isolated tool pack.
Append line to tool audit log file.
Dolly or zoom the editor view.
Read active level editor camera transform and FOV.
Orbit the active level editor camera around a pivot.
Pan the active editor camera.
Pilot a CameraActor or exit pilot mode.
Set editor camera location and rotation.
Schedule viewport capture after N frames.
Capture active editor viewport to PNG on disk.
Frame actors by path using bounds and FOV fit. Actors with no renderable geometry fall back to a small box at their location.
Frame current selection in the viewport.
tools.ue_entry_points: Editor Focus Selected / viewport framing commands

tools.summary: Switch editor viewport rendering mode (Lit, Wireframe, ...).
Emit and persist a structured todo plan (unreal_ai.todo_plan).

Create a new asset under /Game via IAssetTools (asset_class path + optional factory_class). Use for Blueprint assets/materials/sounds/etc. For Character/Pawn gameplay, create a Blueprint asset (/Script/Engine.Blueprint) then set parent_class via blueprint_apply_ir create_if_missing flow.

package_path.description: Long package path folder under /Game, e.g. /Game/AI/Generated

asset_class.description: Native asset UClass path (not content path), e.g. /Script/Engine.Blueprint, /Script/Engine.Material, /Script/LevelSequence.LevelSequence. Do not pass gameplay actor classes like /Script/Engine.Character here.

class_path.description: Compatibility alias for asset_class. Prefer asset_class.

factory_class.description: Optional UFactory class path when the engine requires a factory

focused.description: After success, open the new asset in its editor (uses object_path from the result).

tools.summary: Export editable UObject properties under /Game to JSON (reflection). Prefer before bespoke subsystem reads.
Apply JSON property deltas to an asset under /Game (reflection / ImportText). Use dry_run to validate.

properties.description: Map of property name to JSON value

tools.summary: List Asset Registry referencers for an asset identifier.
List Asset Registry direct dependencies for an asset.
Create a Level Sequence asset under /Game (typed shortcut over asset_create).
Read-only: export Blueprint graph IR for an existing Blueprint asset path (usually discovered via asset_index_fuzzy_search/asset_registry_query). Use before blueprint_apply_ir when patching logic.

blueprint_path.description: Blueprint asset path under /Game. Accepted forms: /Game/Folder/MyBP or /Game/Folder/MyBP.MyBP.

focused.description: After success, open this Blueprint editor (optional graph_name).

tools.summary: Merge structured worker outputs.
